text.title: langchain-chains Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for LCEL chain patterns.

rule.statement: Use `with_structured_output(PydanticModel)` over `JsonOutputParser` for any chain whose downstream depends on specific fields.
rule.rationale: Function calling enforces the schema at API level; JsonOutputParser drifts on smaller models.

rule.statement: Chains MUST be defined at module level, NOT inside request handlers; chain compilation must happen once at import.
rule.rationale: Recreating chains per request wastes CPU and prevents reuse.

rule.statement: Every `with_fallbacks()` call MUST set `exceptions_to_handle` explicitly to the retryable error classes; the default catches everything and masks config bugs.
rule.rationale: Without explicit scoping, auth failures and 4xx errors silently retry to backup providers, hiding the bug.

rule.statement: For true parallel fan-out over a list, use `asyncio.gather` over `chain.ainvoke` per element; `RunnableParallel` with a dict of chains executes synchronously in CPython.
rule.rationale: `RunnableParallel` is parallel-shape but not concurrent runtime without explicit async.

rule.statement: Every chain that calls an external API MUST use `chain.with_retry(stop_after_attempt=3, wait_exponential_jitter=True)`.
rule.rationale: Transient errors are common; explicit retry is required.

rule.statement: RouterBranch MUST have a meaningful default chain as the last argument; conditions are checked sequentially and unmatched inputs MUST NOT silently fail.
rule.rationale: Default fallback prevents silent bugs when none of the conditions match.
